info.title: Jupico - OpenAPI 3.0
info.description: Explore our comprehensive docs for additional resources, including FAQs, best practices, and dedicated support.

tags.name: Status API
tags.description: Monitor the health and performance of the platform to ensure stability and availability.

tags.name: Onboarding Invites API
tags.description: Manage the lifecycle of onboarding invitations, including creation, dispatch, and cancellation, to streamline the process of integrating new users or partners into the system.

tags.name: Card Transactions API
tags.description: Manage card transactions, including tokenization, sales, authorizations, captures, refunds, voids, and reversals.

tags.name: E-Check Transactions API
tags.description: Manage e-check transactions, sales, authorizations, captures, refunds, voids, and reversals.

tags.name: Invoices API
tags.description: Manage invoices throughout their lifecycle, from creation and modification to deletion and distribution, to ensure accurate and timely billing processes.

tags.name: Installments API
tags.description: Manage installment payment plans, including creation, activation, deactivation, deletion, and payment token updates.

tags.name: Dynamic Payouts API
tags.description: Initiate or void payouts to any merchant bank account, at any time. Merchant must have dynamic mode enabled or requests will fail.

tags.name: Query API
tags.description: Utilize flexible querying capabilities, detailed at https://www.npmjs.com/package/query-to-mongo, with support for advanced filters and parameters to retrieve specific data.

tags.name: Provisioning API
tags.description: Manage the merchant onboarding process by creating applicants, applications, and submerchants to enable payment processing capabilities.

tags.name: Currency Exchange API
tags.description: Query live exchange rates, convert amounts between currencies, and retrieve the list of supported currency codes.

post.summary: Create a dynamic payouts for credit cards
Create a dynamic payouts for echeck

post.description: A 'Sale' combines the authorization and capture process into one transaction. It is typically used when customer purchases are fulfilled immediately, e.g grocery store. If the cardholder's issuing bank approves the transaction, an approval code will be returned. If the transaction is declined by the issuing bank for insufficient funds or other reasons, an error message containing the reason is returned.

An 'Authorization' confirms a cardholder's ability to pay and is used when the final transaction amount is unknown or products/services aren't rendered immediately e.g adjusting for tips at a restaraunt or putting a card on file at a hotel.

A 'Capture' is the process of confirming and initiating settlement on a previously authorized transaction, charging the customer for the reserved amount. A Capture can be entered for any amount up to an allowable excess limit, which is determined by the cardholder's issuing bank.
A 'Refund' returns funds to the customer's card for a previously settled transaction. Refunds are typically issued in cases of returns, cancellations, or customer disputes.

A 'Rollback' (or Communications/System Reversal) is generated when there is a problem delivering the response back to the server side code. It is your responsibility, as the original requestor, to generate a 'Rollback' for all transactions that do not receive responses.

An eCheck 'Sale' transaction initiates a direct debit from a customer's bank account. It is used when funds need to be collected immediately, similar to a credit card sale. If the bank approves the debit request, the transaction is completed successfully. Otherwise, an error message indicating the failure reason will be returned.

This operation securely tokenizes a customer’s bank account information. The resulting token can be used in place of raw account data in future transactions, improving security and simplifying compliance.
An eCheck 'Refund' reverses a previously settled debit transaction by returning the funds to the customer's bank account. It is typically used for returns, order cancellations, or customer disputes.

An eCheck 'Rollback' reverses a previously settled debit transaction by returning the funds to the customer's bank account. It is typically used for returns, order cancellations, or customer disputes.
Obtain a chronological timeline of transactions, providing a detailed view of transaction activities over time.

value.transactionId: Id of the transaction to get timeline

Create an invite to onboard a new merchant or partner, without sending it immediately.

name.description: The name of the person being invited.

email.description: The email of the person being invited.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the person being invited.

identity.description: The identity object containing data about the business and its representatives

feesTemplateId.description: UUID of the fees template with predefined fees. If no value is passed, the id of the default template will be used.

fees.description: The fees object will overwrite the values set on the fees template, as specified by the feesTemplateId. This allows for customization of fees at the invite overriding any defaults defined in the template.

onboardingFlowTemplateId.description: UUID of the onboarding flow template to be used for the onboarding experience.

post.description: Set up a new plan, under an existing subscription.

subMerchantId.description: The subMerchantId associated with the plan.

items.description: Array of billing numbers day[0-32] for monthly, 0 at period start(month's 1st), 32 at period end(month's last day)

type.description: Pricing model type, one of ["free", "flat", "flatTiered", "perModule", "perUsageQuantity", "perUsageVolume", "perUsageTiered"]. Depending of this value you must provide corresponding next atribute.

hostedSession.description: Object to edit configuration of created hosted session.

enabled.description: Only required field, setting enabled = true, we create a session with default values. In case you want customization, append below data.

timeLimit.description: Number of minutes to keep session active

type.description: Usage type of hosted session. Posible values ["single_use","multi_use"]

quantity.description: In case of usage type "multi_use", this attribute update the hosted session payments/subscriptions limit.

post.description: Set up a new installment payment plan for a customer. When created, the initial charge depends on the deposit amount and start date: if no deposit and no future start date, the first payment is charged immediately; if a future start date is set with no deposit, only card validation is performed; if a deposit is provided with no future start date, the deposit plus first payment are charged immediately; if a deposit is provided with a future start date, only the deposit is charged immediately.

subMerchantId.description: The subMerchantId associated with the installment.

customerId.description: The ID of the customer for this installment.

totalAmount.description: The total amount to be paid across all installments.

deposit.description: Optional deposit charged at creation time.

amount.description: Deposit amount. Use 0 for no deposit.

startDate.description: Optional first installment date in YYYY-MM-DD format. If omitted or today, first payment is charged immediately.

post.description: Updates an existing application. Note: The application must be in 'review' status to be updated. Once activated, it cannot be modified.

Updates an existing submerchant. Note: The submerchant must be in 'review' status to be updated. Once activated, it cannot be modified. All fields in identity.business are optional. feesTemplateId and payoutBankAccounts are optional, but if payoutBankAccounts is provided, accountRouting, accountNumber, accountType, and nameOnAccount are required.

subMerchantId.description: Submerchant identifier
Unique identifier for the submerchant

post.description: Returns exchange rates for a given base currency. If symbols is omitted, all supported currencies are returned.
Converts an amount from one currency to another. convertedAmount is rounded down to 2 decimal places.
Returns the list of all supported currency codes, sorted alphabetically.

metadata.description: metadata is an object that can host as much as 50 key-value pairs, where the keys can be at most 40 char length and can’t include the characters “[“ or “]”, and the values must be strings and cannot exceed 500 chars [Learn more about metadata](https://docs.jupico.com/docs/metadata#/)

taxAmount.description:
The total amount of sales tax must be between:
Visa: 0.1% - 18%
MasterCard: 0.1% - 23%
Default: 0.1% - 50%

name.description: The text that appears on the cardholder's statement, overriding the default business name.

Default percentage: 0.1% - 50%

position.description:
The position of the owner. Possible values are:
- OW: Owner
- CEO: Chief Executive Officer
- CFO: Chief Financial Officer
- COO: Chief Operating Officer
- PRESIDENT: President
- VICE PRESIDENT: Vice President
- DIR: Director
- TR: Treasurer
- SEC: Secretary
- PA: Partner
- MEMBER LLC: Member of LLC
- OTH: Other
- SP: Sole Proprietorship

tinType.description:
The type of Tax Identification Number (TIN):
- SSN (Social Security Number)
- EIN (Employer Identification Number)
- Other
